# terrible_netapp_ontap_na_ontap_vscan_on_access_policy (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `policy_name` (String) The name of the policy
- `vserver` (String) the name of the data vserver to use.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `file_ext_to_exclude` (String) File extensions for which On-Access scanning must not be performed.
- `file_ext_to_include` (String) File extensions for which On-Access scanning is considered. The default value is '*', which means that all files are considered for scanning except those which are excluded from scanning.
- `filters` (String) A list of filters which can be used to define the scope of the On-Access policy more precisely. The filters can be added in any order. Possible values scan_ro_volume  Enable scans for read-only volume, scan_execute_access  Scan only files opened with execute-access (CIFS only). deprecated with REST, use `scan_readonly_volumes` or `only_execute_access`.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `is_scan_mandatory` (Boolean) Specifies whether access to a file is allowed if there are no external virus-scanning servers available for virus scanning. If not specified, default value is False in ZAPI.
- `max_file_size` (Number) Max file-size (in bytes) allowed for scanning. The default value of 2147483648 (2GB) is taken if not provided at the time of creating a policy.
- `only_execute_access` (Boolean) Scan only files opened with execute-access. If not specified, default value is False in creating policy.
- `paths_to_exclude` (String) File paths for which On-Access scanning must not be performed.
- `policy_status` (Boolean) Status for the created policy
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `scan_files_with_no_ext` (Boolean) Specifies whether files without any extension are considered for scanning or not. If not specified, default value is True in ZAPI.
- `scan_readonly_volumes` (Boolean) Specifies whether or not read-only volume can be scanned. If not specified, default value is False in creating policy.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Whether a Vscan on Access policy is present or not
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
